llm_result.summary: This case centers on appetite lability with nocturnal eating driven by accessible late-night foods, a cycle of hunger, binge-like episodes around midnight, and punitive self-talk the next day. The proposed levers span biological (sleep circadian alignment, caffeine/light exposure, meal timing), psychological (emotion regulation, flexible dieting, mindful eating), and social/environmental (household food-access controls, social support) domains, including cross-domain feedback and monitoring. Optimal impact comes from coordinated changes that reduce late-night opportunities, stabilize hunger signals, dampen post-episode distress, and promote sustainable routines. All predictors are modifiable and non-diagnostic, designed to be feasible in a typical primary-care or coaching context with simple metrics for progress over 2–6 weeks.

solutions.predictor_variable: Structured meal timing and portion-controlled meals; regular protein-rich meals with planned evening snack
solutions.solution_candidate: Implement a daily meal template with fixed eating windows (e.g., 7–8am, 12–1pm, 6–7pm), ensure each meal includes a source of protein, and add a planned, modest evening snack to reduce hunger spikes at night. Pair with brief psychoeducation on hunger cues and self-monitoring.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Stabilizes inter-meal hunger/desire fluctuations and lowers the likelihood of abrupt midnight eating by providing predictable intake and reducing nocturnal cravings; also supports self-efficacy through structure.
solutions.measurement: Daily hunger rating (0–10); number of midnight eating episodes per week; percentage adherence to scheduled meals; total nighttime caloric intake.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP:S01 -> targets C01 and C02; meal timing as lever for appetite and nocturnal eating.

solutions.predictor_variable: Sleep regularization and circadian alignment (consistent bed/wake times, evening light exposure management, caffeine timing)
solutions.solution_candidate: Set fixed wake time (e.g., 7–8am) and bedtime (e.g., 10–11pm); create a wind-down routine with dim lighting 60 minutes before bed; limit bright screens; cap caffeine after 2pm; minimize naps; track sleep with a simple diary or wearable.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved sleep continuity and timely melatonin signaling reduce nocturnal arousals and opportunities for late-night eating; enhanced executive control during the day supports better impulse management.
solutions.measurement: Bedtime/wake times; sleep onset latency; total sleep time (objective or diary); number of nocturnal eating episodes; self-reported sleep quality.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP:S02 -> targets C06 (sleep) and C02 (night-time eating frequency) via sleep improvement.

solutions.solution_candidate: Create household rules to reduce late-night food accessibility: remove high-risk snacks from bedrooms, implement a kitchen closing time, store tempting foods out of sight or behind locked cabinets, and designate a single, limited late-evening snack window.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Decreases cue-driven late-night intake by reducing availability and cue exposure, thereby lowering binge-like episodes and reducing overall consumption at night.
solutions.measurement: Number of snacks accessible after dinner; number of midnight eating episodes; subjective accessibility ratings.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP:S03 -> targets C02 (nighttime eating) and C05 (accessibility) through environmental controls.

solutions.predictor_variable: Post-eating cognitive-emotional regulation: self-compassion and cognitive reframing
solutions.solution_candidate: Provide brief, daily practice in self-compassion and cognitive reframing after eating episodes (e.g., compassionate self-talk, urge-surfing statements, journaling prompts to reframe episode as data, not moral failure).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces post-episode shame and self-criticism that perpetuate restrictive dieting and subsequent overeating; improves affective regulation and resilience.
solutions.measurement: Post-episode mood ratings; frequency of self-criticism statements; use of self-compassion phrases; journaling completion rate.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP:S04 -> target C03 (post-eating negative self-evaluation) through emotion regulation.

solutions.solution_candidate: Replace all-or-nothing dieting language with a flexible, values-aligned daily choices framework; implement a 1-piece-of-advice-per-day approach to reduce punitive thinking (e.g., "today I choose nourishing options, and occasional indulgences fit within a balanced plan").
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces the punitive cycle that worsens binge/purge risk by normalizing occasional deviations and emphasizing sustainable self-regulation.
solutions.measurement: Frequency of 'be good tomorrow' statements; daily rule adherence; mood after meals.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP:S05 -> target C04 (rigid dieting intent) via cognitive restructuring.

solutions.predictor_variable: Evening light exposure and wind-down optimization
solutions.solution_candidate: Implement an evening routine with dim lighting, 30–60 minutes of quiet activity (reading/stretching), and restricted bright screen use to support circadian timing and sleep pressure.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Promotes earlier sleep onset and reduces late-night wakefulness, thereby lowering opportunities for nocturnal eating.
solutions.measurement: Evening screen time; time to sleep onset; total sleep duration; nocturnal eating episodes.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP:S06 -> target C06 (sleep) to reduce nocturnal eating opportunities.

solutions.solution_candidate: Limit caffeine after 2pm and track total daily caffeine; correlate with sleep quality and nocturnal awakenings.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Caffeine can disrupt sleep and increase nocturnal arousal; reducing late intake lowers wakefulness and opportunities for night eating.
solutions.measurement: Caffeine intake diary; subjective sleep quality; nocturnal awakenings; midnight eating episodes.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP:S07 -> targets C06 and C02 via caffeine sleep linkage.

solutions.predictor_variable: Mindful eating training and satiety cue recognition
solutions.solution_candidate: Teach mindful eating skills (slowed pace, hunger/fullness checks, savoring) at meals to improve interoceptive awareness and satiety signaling; practice reducing impulse to snack before bed.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhances recognition of true hunger cues and reduces impulsive snacking, including nighttime eating.
solutions.measurement: Eating pace (minutes per bite); fullness ratings post-meal; number of night snacks.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP:S08 -> targets C01 (appetite lability) and C02 (night eating) via mindful eating.

solutions.solution_candidate: Engage an accountability partner for meal planning, check-ins, and progress tracking; optionally join a lightweight online group focused on sleep/eating patterns for support and perspective.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Social support enhances adherence, reduces isolation, and buffers negative affect that can trigger binge episodes; improves post-episode coping.
solutions.measurement: Frequency of check-ins; perceived social support scale; number of midnight eating episodes; post-meal mood.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP:S09 -> targets C01, C02, C03 via social support and accountability.

solutions.solution_candidate: Use a simple journaling/app to track hunger (0–10), mood (0–10), sleep quality, and midnight eating episodes; receive weekly summary feedback and plan adjustments.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Data-driven feedback increases self-awareness, detects triggers, and enables dynamic, timely adjustments to routines and coping skills.
solutions.measurement: Weekly trend scores for hunger, mood, sleep, and midnight eating; adherence to logging.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP:S10 -> targets C01, C02, C03, C06 via integrated bio-psycho-social monitoring.
